title: How Much Does It Cost to Rent a Yacht? A Worldwide Price Guide
topic: How much does it cost to rent a yacht? A worldwide price guide
html:
Renting a yacht is far more accessible than most people imagine, but prices swing widely depending on the boat, the destination and the season. A half-day on a small motor yacht might cost less than a nice dinner for four, while a week aboard a fully crewed superyacht can run into six figures. This guide breaks down what actually drives the price, so you can set a realistic budget before you rent a boat for your next trip.

What you are really paying for

The headline rate is only part of the picture. Yacht hire is priced around a handful of core factors, and understanding them helps you compare quotes fairly.

  • Size and type: length in metres or feet is the single biggest driver. A 10 metre dayboat and a 30 metre motor yacht sit in completely different price brackets.
  • Crew: a skipper, and on larger vessels a hostess, chef and deckhands, add daily wages on top of the base rate.
  • Season: peak summer months in the Mediterranean or the Caribbean high season command a premium. Shoulder seasons can be a third cheaper.
  • Location: glamorous hotspots like the French Riviera or the Amalfi Coast cost more than quieter, less fashionable coastlines.
  • Age and luxury level: a brand new yacht with the latest toys and finishes prices higher than an older but well kept boat.

Typical price ranges by boat type

These are broad worldwide averages for guidance only. Real quotes vary by region, but they give you a useful sense of scale when you start to browse boats online.

Day charters

  • Small motor yacht or RIB (up to 10m): roughly 300 to 900 pounds for a half or full day, often self drive or with a local skipper.
  • Sailing yacht or catamaran (10 to 15m): around 600 to 1,800 pounds per day, skipper usually included or optional.
  • Mid size motor yacht (15 to 24m): typically 2,000 to 6,000 pounds per day with crew.

Weekly charters

  • Bareboat sailing yacht (you sail it yourself): from about 1,500 pounds a week in low season up to 8,000 pounds for a large, modern boat in peak months.
  • Crewed catamaran: commonly 10,000 to 30,000 pounds per week including crew.
  • Luxury motor yacht (24m and above): 50,000 pounds to several hundred thousand per week, with the largest superyachts going far beyond that.

The extras that catch people out

On larger crewed yachts especially, the advertised rate rarely includes everything. Budget for these before you commit to a yacht charter.

  1. Fuel: on bigger boats this is often charged separately and can be significant on a fast motor yacht.
  2. Advance Provisioning Allowance: crewed charters typically add around 20 to 30 percent of the base rate to cover food, drink, fuel and berthing.
  3. Marina and mooring fees: overnight berths in popular ports add up quickly.
  4. Crew gratuity: a tip of 5 to 15 percent is customary on a crewed yacht.
  5. Security deposit: refundable, but you need it available at the time of booking.

How to keep the cost down

You do not need a celebrity budget to enjoy time on the water. A few simple choices make a real difference.

  • Travel in shoulder season, when weather is still good but rates drop.
  • Split the cost across a group: per person, a day on a catamaran can be very reasonable.
  • Choose a self drive boat where your licence and local rules allow, avoiding crew wages.
  • Book a day trip rather than an overnight charter if you mainly want sun, swimming and a sense of freedom.
  • Compare several listings side by side rather than taking the first quote you see.

The easiest way to gauge a fair price is to look at live availability across destinations. You can hire a boat in hundreds of locations and see transparent pricing for each one in a single place.

A quick word for boat owners

If you already own a yacht that sits unused for much of the year, those same charter rates are an opportunity. Owners can list your boat and offset running costs by renting it out to travellers when they are not using it themselves.

The honest answer to how much a yacht costs is that there is a price point for almost everyone, from an affordable afternoon on a small boat to a once in a lifetime week with full crew. Decide on the experience first, then match the budget to the boat.
